steps.title: Use the circle equation form
steps.content: A circle with center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$ has equation $$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2.$$

steps.title: Substitute the center and radius
steps.content: The center is $(16,17)$ and the radius is $7k$, so $$(x-16)^2+(y-17)^2=(7k)^2.$$

steps.title: Avoid the radius-squared trap
steps.content: The right side of a circle equation is the radius squared. Since the radius is $7k$, the right side is $(7k)^2=49k^2$, not $7k$ or $49k$.
